question_frequency.rationale: The Pair asks essentially no didactic questions. Turn 1 just dumps the full solution; Turn 2 presents two approaches but doesn't actually pose a choice to the user — it shows both implementations and editorializes which is better.

question_quality.rationale: The closed-form vs iteration contrast is a legitimate teachable fork, but the Pair never frames it as a real choice — it just narrates both and tells the user which to internalize. The `//` vs `/` integer-division note is genuine but presented as trivia, not a question.

scaffolding.rationale: Turn 1 spoils the entire answer (25,164,150) before any conversation happens, eliminating any chance for scaffolded discovery. There is no progression — it's a one-shot solution dump followed by code.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and the prose is clear, but the dynamic is off: the 'user' (Haiku) ends up doing the didactic framing in Turn 2, and the Pair just confirms. It reads as parallel monologues rather than back-and-forth.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
